description.value: Selects allow you to choose items from a menu of predefined options.
Use the label attribute to give the select an accessible label. For labels that contain HTML, use the label slot instead.

Add descriptive help text to a switch with the help-text attribute. For help texts that contain HTML, use the help-text slot instead.
The help-text attribute should not be used to display error messages. To handle validation and error messaging, use syn-validate for proper error management.
Use the clearable attribute to make the control clearable. The clear button only appears when an option is selected.
Use the disabled attribute to disable a select.
Add the readonly attribute to a select to draw it in a readonly state.

To allow multiple options to be selected, use the multiple attribute. It’s a good practice to use clearable when this option is enabled. To set multiple values at once, set value to a space-delimited list of values.

Use the value attribute to set the initial selection.

When using multiple, the value attribute uses space-delimited values to select more than one option. Because of this, <syn-option> values cannot contain spaces. If you’re accessing the value property through Javascript, it will be an array.
Use <syn-optgroup> to group listbox items visually.
Use the size attribute to change a select’s size. Note that size does not apply to listbox options.
Use the prefix and suffix slots to add text and icons.

When multiple options can be selected, you can provide custom tags by passing a function to the getTag property. Your function can return a string of HTML, a Lit Template, or an HTMLElement. The getTag() function will be called for each option. The first argument is an <syn-option> element and the second argument is the tag’s index (its position in the tag list).

Remember that custom tags are rendered in a shadow root. To style them, you can use the style attribute in your template or you can add your own parts and target them with the ::part() selector.
Use the placeholder attribute to add a placeholder.
The focus event gives the user feedback that the Select has been focused by the keyboard interaction or active click from the user..
The invalid status is used to warn the user that the Select is invalid. For example, if the entry of text is mandatory and nothing has been entered.

accessibility: If multiple options can be selected, clearly announce this capability to screenreader users and offer a way to view all chosen items.
Be aware that group labels in select components will be neglected by most assistive devices.

content: Use light background options such as white, neutral-100, or primary-100.
usageGuidelines.name: Background

content: Keep each option to a single line of text for readability.
Ensure all options have consistent line lengths for easier scanning.
Avoid using the same word or phrase at the beginning of options.
Provide a meaningful placeholder option to guide users.
usageGuidelines.name: Content and Labels

content: Use for lists with more than five options; for fewer options, consider using syn-radio for single selection or syn-checkbox for multiple selection.
Apply the "multiple" attribute when multiple selections are allowed, and pair it with "checkbox" for corresponding nested options.
By default, the number of selected options is displayed after the text.
Display selected options as tags within the field to help users track their selections.
Consider limiting the maximum number of displayed selectable options to avoid overwhelming users. We recommend displaying 6-8 (with scrolling for additional results).
usageGuidelines.name: Selection Behavior

useCases: Enable users to select one or more options from a list of predefined choices in forms.
Implement in filter panels to allow users to refine search results or data views.
Provide options in settings pages where users need to choose preferences from a list.
Long or descriptive labels, helper text, or units are needed.
Forms with many fields that users must scan quickly.
Complex fields with adornments, counters, or tooltips.
Short labels (1-3 words); minimal helper text.
You want a clean look without duplicate placeholder text.

custom.summary: Selects allow you to choose items from a menu of predefined options.
